semanticCore: Chewing, chewed lump, fetus stage, mouthful, or muscle
quranicRelevance: Embryo developing into chewed lump (Mudghah - 'min mudhghatin mukhallaqatin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Chewed lump of flesh, early embryo stage, mouthful, or small mass

figurativeOrRareUsages: ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ مُضْغَةٍ مُخَلَّقَةٍ وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ (22:5): Then from a clinging clot, then from a chewed lump of flesh (Mudghah), formed and unformed (embryology sequence).
discardedNoise: Chewing gum (Mudgh) in oral health brochures.
The heart piece Hadith ('إذا صلحت صلح الجسد كله') - post-canonical hadith text.
summaryEn: The root represents chewed lumps (Mudghah), serving as a precise biological description of the early embryo's segmented, teeth-like shape.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر مضغ الطعام وقطعة اللحم الصغيرة بقدر ما يمضغ (مضغة)، واصفاً المرحلة الجنينية الثالثة لتطور هيكل اللحم والفقار (مضغة مخلقة).
rawText: مُضْغَةٌ A piece, or bit, of flesh (T, S, K), &c.: (T, K:) or a morsel, or gobbet, of flesh, i. e. a piece of flesh such as a man puts into his mouth: (Khálid Ibn-Jembeh, TA:) or as much as is chewed [at once]: (Msb:) and such as the heart, and the tongue, of a man: (TA:) and a fœtus when it has become like a lump of flesh: see Kur. xxii. 5; and see خَلِيقٌ.
